qualifications.name: Bachelor of Engineering (BEng) (Chemical Engineering / Electrical and Electronic Engineering / Computer and Electronic Engineering / Mechanical Engineering / Industrial Engineering / Mechatronic Engineering)
notes: Field: Chemical Engineering / Electrical and Electronic Engineering / Computer and Electronic Engineering / Mechanical Engineering / Industrial Engineering / Mechatronic Engineering.

Additional NWU requirements: Afrikaans or English mark of 60% or higher at Home Language or First Additional Language level; Prospective students who apply with borderline Grade 11 results may be conditionally accepted, provided they meet the minimum requirements in their final Grade 12 results; Applicants with borderline Grade 11 results are encouraged to choose a second study option from a faculty other than Engineering; Students with 40% or higher in both Mathematics and Physical Sciences and at least 60% in the language of instruction can apply for the 1-year Xcel bridging programme.
